prompt: A vertically oriented literary poster in a muted sepia, ash, and parchment palette with dramatic gold kintsugi-style cracks running diagonally across the entire composition. The background is a soft textured paper and watercolor wash with smoky clouds, worn stains, and faint marbling. In the upper left, place a large faded monochrome portrait bust of an older Indian man in traditional formal attire, partially emerging from the mist like a historical figure or poet, softly blended into the background. In the upper right, set a small Hindi title line परशुराम की प्रतीक्षा with a smaller author credit below it, elegant and understated. Beneath that, place one large centered Hindi quote in refined serif-style Devanagari, framed by oversized quotation marks, using the exact text जब नाश मनुज पर छाता है, पहले विवेक मर जाता है।. In the lower half, create a heroic mythic Indian warrior standing on jagged black rocks, facing slightly left, with a powerful windswept stance. He has a bare muscular torso, long wild dark hair blown upward, and a tattered deep red drape wrapped across his body and trailing dramatically to the right in torn ribbons. He holds 1 large battle axe in his left hand, the blade angled downward, suggesting Parashurama. Surround him with dust, smoke, tiny debris, and painterly splashes. Add 2 vertical blocks of smaller Hindi poem text, one on the lower left and one on the lower right of the warrior, balanced like a printed poetry poster. Keep the poem text dense and readable but secondary to the central quote and figure. Use painterly digital illustration with fine art poster aesthetics, soft atmospheric lighting, high contrast around the warrior, and subtle glowing gold fissures crossing through the portrait, text area, rocks, and fabric. The overall mood is solemn, prophetic, patriotic, and intense, like a shareable inspirational poem poster about moral collapse and righteous awakening in India.
